headline: Anthropic dominated the week with Sonnet 5, a science research app, and Teams and Slack integrations as agent technique slugs broke out across the board.
body: Anthropic shipped Claude Sonnet 5, which closes the benchmark gap with Opus 4.8, and launched Claude Science, a reproducibility-focused research app covering genomics, structural biology, and cheminformatics. On the technique side, trajectory data, Gradient Staleness, vulnerability detection, behavioral auditing, ablation, and semantic chunking all led and broke out simultaneously, with agent skills and parallel execution registering as new entrants. Google Cloud pushed continuous evaluation pipelines for multi-agent systems using shadow deployments and tool-use quality metrics, while OpenAI's Greg Brockman framed compute scaling and persistent agents as the route to personal AGI. Cost dynamics also surfaced through analysis of tokenmaxxing and test-time compute driving rising AI prices.

cited_items.title: Sonnet 5 is LIVE And It Competes With Opus
